title: Captains Courageous

memes.label: "Anchor"
memes.description: "The heavy iron device that holds a schooner fast to the sea floor — on the Grand Banks, where the bottom shifts with every tide, the anchor and the skill of anchoring are matters of survival as much as convenience."

memes.label: Bait
memes.description: Bait is essential to cod fishing on the Grand Banks in Captains Courageous. Fishermen use salt clams and fish offal to lure cod onto their hooks, and the preparation of bait is a central part of the daily fishing routine.

memes.label: Bank Law
memes.description: The informal but strictly enforced system of regulations governing fishing conduct on the Grand Banks, including hook limits, territory rights, and dory etiquette

memes.label: "Ben Ireson"
memes.description: "Benjamin 'Ben' Ireson, the skipper of the schooner Betty, was a real Marblehead captain whose story of alleged cowardice was immortalized in Whittier's poem 'Skipper Ireson's Ride' — and whose reputation is vigorously defended by Tom Platt in Captains Courageous."

memes.label: Boom
memes.description: The boom is a horizontal spar attached to the foot of a sail on the fishing schooners of Captains Courageous. The foreboom and other booms are key structural elements that control the sails and shape the working deck of a Grand Banks vessel.

memes.label: "Boston"
memes.description: "The principal port city of Massachusetts, home port of many Grand Banks fishing schooners including the We're Here, and the destination that Harvey Cheyne's father races toward across the continent in the novel's climax."

memes.label: Bow
memes.description: The bow is the forward part of a vessel, a critical structural element of the fishing schooners in Captains Courageous. The bow cuts through the waves and houses essential deck equipment, and the bowsprit extends forward to carry the headsails.

memes.label: Buoy
memes.description: Buoys are floating markers used by the Grand Banks fishermen in Captains Courageous to mark the location of their trawl lines and anchor cables. Small painted trawl-buoys with flags are essential for finding gear in the fog and open ocean.

memes.label: "Cabin"
memes.description: "The low, cramped living quarters below the quarter-deck of a Grand Banks fishing schooner — the only sheltered space aboard, serving as galley, bunkroom, and meeting place for the crew."

memes.label: California
memes.description: A U.S. state on the Pacific coast, significant in the novel as the home of Cheyne's business empire, the setting for his rail operations, and the destination toward which the transcontinental search for Harvey mobilizes.

memes.label: "Cape Breton"
memes.description: "Cape Breton Island, at the northeastern tip of Nova Scotia — a rugged fishing region that supplies crewmen to the Grand Banks fleet and appears in the speech, songs, and geography of Captains Courageous."

memes.label: Cape Cod
memes.description: A prominent hook-shaped peninsula and geographic landmark on the Massachusetts coast, known for its lighthouse, fishing heritage, and as a navigational reference point for vessels approaching Boston and Gloucester.

memes.label: "Caplin"
memes.description: "A small silvery forage fish of the North Atlantic that migrates in vast schools, used as bait by Grand Banks cod fishermen and celebrated in the novel as the occasion for a spectacular nighttime fishing frenzy."

memes.label: Captain
memes.description: The captain, or skipper, is the commanding officer of a fishing vessel in Captains Courageous. Disko Troop, the captain of the We're Here, embodies the authority, knowledge, and responsibility of the role on the Grand Banks.

memes.label: Carrie Pitman
memes.description: A Gloucester fishing schooner in the North Atlantic fleet, sister ship to the We're Here and known among fishermen for her poor luck.

memes.label: Catholic Faith
memes.description: The Roman Catholic faith practiced by the Portuguese fishermen in the Gloucester fleet, contrasted with the Protestant culture of the New England fishing community.

memes.label: "I"
memes.description: "Harvey Cheyne falls overboard"

memes.label: "II"
memes.description: "Aboard the We're Here"

memes.label: "III"
memes.description: "Life on the fishing schooner"

memes.label: "IV"
memes.description: "The crew and their ways"

memes.label: "V"
memes.description: "Fishing on the Grand Banks"

memes.label: "VI"
memes.description: "The We're Here fleet"

memes.label: "VII"
memes.description: "Growing into a fisherman"

memes.label: "VIII"
memes.description: "The fog and the wreck"

memes.label: "IX"
memes.description: "Return to Gloucester"

memes.label: "X"
memes.description: "Reunion and transformation"

memes.label: "Cheyne Business Empire"
memes.description: "The vast railroad and industrial fortune built by Harvey Cheyne Sr. — a self-made California millionaire whose wealth represents the Gilded Age's scale of capital, and whose relationship with his son is transformed by the boy's experiences aboard the We're Here."

memes.label: "Cheyne Railroad"
memes.description: "The railroad empire of Harvey Cheyne Sr. — a web of Western lines whose boardroom battles and transcontinental reach symbolize the Gilded Age's scale of industrial capital in Captains Courageous."

memes.label: The Cheyne Reunion
memes.description: The emotional reunion of Harvey Cheyne with his parents in Gloucester, Massachusetts, after the boy has been presumed dead — orchestrated through a transcontinental railroad dash in the private car *Constance*.

memes.label: Chicago
memes.description: Harvey Cheyne's home city and the hub of the railroad network through which his father orchestrates the cross-continental rescue effort in Chapter IX.

memes.label: "Cod Fishing"
memes.description: "The primary economic activity aboard Grand Banks fishing schooners in the late 19th century — hand-lining for Atlantic cod from small dories, followed by splitting, salting, and packing the catch in the vessel's hold."

memes.label: Compass
memes.description: The compass is a navigational instrument essential to the fishing schooners of Captains Courageous, used by the crew to determine direction on the featureless Grand Banks where no landmarks guide the way.

memes.label: Index of Concepts
memes.description: Complete index of all nautical, cultural, and thematic concepts in the Captains Courageous wiki, organized alphabetically.

memes.label: "Conch"
memes.description: "A large spiral shell used as a signaling horn by Grand Banks dory fishermen, blown to communicate position through the thick Bank fog and to summon help when lost or in danger."

memes.label: Constance (Private Car)
memes.description: Mr. Cheyne's luxurious private railroad car, named after his wife, which transports the family from California to Gloucester upon Harvey's return.

memes.label: Constance Cheyne
memes.description: Constance Cheyne (Mrs. Cheyne), Harvey's mother, whose grief-stricken collapse and frantic journey across America mirror her husband's race to rescue their son.

memes.label: Cook
memes.description: The cook is a vital member of a fishing schooner's crew in Captains Courageous, responsible for feeding the men. The We're Here carries a distinctive African-American cook whose presence and meals anchor the daily rhythm of shipboard life.

memes.label: Courage
memes.description: Courage is a central theme of Captains Courageous, appearing in the novel's title and embodied by the fishermen of the Grand Banks who face the perils of the sea with steady resolve. The novel explores courage not as reckless daring but as quiet competence and resourcefulness under pressure.

memes.label: "Crew"
memes.description: "The eight or ten men who work a Grand Banks fishing schooner — a tight-knit community of diverse origins, bound together by hard labour, shared danger, and the rhythms of the fishing season."

memes.label: The Cross-Country Dash
memes.description: The transcontinental railroad journey of the Cheyne family from San Diego to Gloucester in their private car *Constance*, orchestrated with industrial precision to reach Harvey after news of his survival — a display of Gilded Age wealth, technology, and parental devotion.

memes.label: "Dan Troop"
memes.description: "The teenage son of Captain Disko Troop and Harvey Cheyne's reluctant mentor aboard the We're Here — an experienced young fisherman who has worked the Grand Banks since childhood and who teaches Harvey the practical skills of fishing."

memes.label: "Deck"
memes.description: "The open working platform of a Grand Banks fishing schooner — a cluttered, wet, and perpetually busy space where cod are dressed down, gear is stowed, and the crew's daily life unfolds above the cabin below."

memes.label: "Disko Troop"
memes.description: "Captain and owner of the fishing schooner We're Here of Gloucester — a skilled, gruff, and fundamentally fair man who knows the Grand Banks as intimately as his own hands and treats all men by the measure of their work rather than their wealth."

memes.label: "Dory"
memes.description: "A small, flat-bottomed fishing boat used by the Grand Banks fishing fleet in the late 19th century, central to the daily operations aboard the We're Here and the setting for Harvey Cheyne's transformation from pampered passenger to working fisherman."

memes.label: "Dory Roding"
memes.description: "A long, thin anchor line — typically seventy fathoms of brown rope — carried in the bow of every Grand Banks fishing dory, used to anchor the boat over productive fishing ground."

memes.label: Drowning
memes.description: The ever-present threat of death by water in the North Atlantic fishing trade, and the transformative experience of near-drowning that reshapes Harvey Cheyne's character

memes.label: East Gloucester
memes.description: East Gloucester, the neighborhood of Gloucester, Massachusetts, where Disko Troop's family lives and where Harvey Cheyne returns to visit after his transformative summer at sea in Captains Courageous.

memes.label: Eastern Point
memes.description: A prominent coastal landmark at the entrance to Gloucester Harbor, serving as a key navigational reference for returning fishing vessels.

memes.label: Eastport
memes.description: A fishing port in eastern Maine whose fishermen appear in *Captains Courageous* at the Gloucester memorial service and in the auction of a dead French deckhand's kit, representing the Down East contingent of the Grand Banks fleet.

memes.label: Index of Events
memes.description: Complete index of all narrative events in the Captains Courageous wiki, sorted alphabetically.

memes.label: Fire Island
memes.description: A barrier island off Long Island, New York, referenced in *Captains Courageous* as the location of legendary pirate treasure guarded by spirits — part of the supernatural folklore of the New England fishing fleet.

memes.label: Fishermen
memes.description: The men of the North Atlantic cod-fishing fleet, who work the Grand Banks from schooners and dories under harsh conditions, forming a distinct maritime community bound by shared labor, tradition, and oral culture

memes.label: Fishing Life
memes.description: The daily routine and way of life aboard a Gloucester fishing schooner on the Grand Banks, defined by hard physical labor, communal living, and the rhythms of the sea.
aliases: daily life aboard a fishing schooner

memes.label: "Fishing on the Grand Banks"
memes.description: "An analytical overview of the Grand Banks cod fishery as depicted in Captains Courageous — its methods, economy, dangers, and role in shaping the characters' lives."

memes.label: "Fog"
memes.description: "A dense, persistent sea fog that blankets the Grand Banks, creating dangerous navigation conditions and serving as a constant atmospheric presence throughout the novel — simultaneously a hazard, a teacher, and a defining element of the Banks' character."

memes.label: Fog on the Banks
memes.description: The dense, recurring fog banks of the Grand Banks — a defining feature of the fishing grounds that serves as both navigational hazard and strategic cover in *Captains Courageous*, shaping the daily life and tactics of the fishing fleet.

memes.label: The Frenchman's Auction
memes.description: The auction at sea of a deceased French deckhand's belongings, held by his Breton captain after the sailor was killed by a wave — a grim but pragmatic maritime tradition witnessed by the *We're Here*'s crew.
aliases: Auction of the Dead Frenchman's Kit

memes.label: Gale
memes.description: A strong wind at sea, representing both the physical danger faced by Grand Banks fishermen and a test of seamanship that separates the competent from the pretender.

memes.label: "Galley"
memes.description: "The tiny shipboard kitchen of a Grand Banks fishing schooner — a cramped, stove-heated space where the cook prepares meals for a crew of eight to twelve men, and where the social life of the vessel centers around food and warmth."

memes.label: "Georges Bank"
memes.description: "A large, productive fishing bank east of Cape Cod — warmer and shallower than the Grand Banks — where Gloucester schooners fish for cod and halibut when the Grand Banks season slows."

memes.label: "Gloucester"
memes.description: "The historic fishing port on Cape Ann, Massachusetts — home port of the We're Here and the center of the New England salt cod fishery in the late 19th century, where the novel's journey begins and ends."

memes.label: Gloucester Fleet
memes.description: The fishing fleet of Gloucester, Massachusetts, as depicted in Captains Courageous — the premier dory-schooner fleet of the Grand Banks fishery.

memes.label: "Grand Banks"
memes.description: "The vast underwater plateau southeast of Newfoundland — one of the world's richest fishing grounds, where the cold Labrador Current meets the warm Gulf Stream, and the primary fishing destination of the Gloucester schooner fleet in Captains Courageous."

memes.label: "Grand Banks Fishing Fleet"
memes.description: "The informal but tightly-knit community of schooners that fish the Grand Banks — a floating society with its own rules, hierarchies, and traditions, bound together by shared danger and mutual dependence."

memes.label: "Gurry"
memes.description: "The discarded fish heads, entrails, and offal produced during the dressing-down of the day's cod catch, collected in a pen on the schooner's deck and later dumped overboard as chum to attract more fish."

memes.label: "Halibut"
memes.description: "A large, highly prized flatfish of the North Atlantic, caught alongside cod on the Grand Banks. Harvey Cheyne's first major catch — a halibut that fights him for twenty minutes — marks his initiation into the community of working fishermen."

memes.label: Halifax
memes.description: The capital of Nova Scotia and a key port city in the Canadian Maritime Provinces, home to many fishermen who work the Grand Banks alongside the Gloucester fleet.

memes.label: "Harvey Cheyne"
memes.description: "The fifteen-year-old protagonist of Captains Courageous — a spoiled, arrogant son of a California railroad millionaire who is washed overboard from a transatlantic liner, rescued by Grand Banks fishermen, and transformed by months of hard physical labor aboard the fishing schooner We're Here."

memes.label: "Harvey Cheyne's Transformation"
memes.description: "A cross-chapter analysis of Harvey Cheyne's moral and psychological transformation from spoiled millionaire's son to competent young fisherman — the novel's central narrative arc."

memes.label: "Harvey Falls Overboard"
memes.description: "The inciting incident of Captains Courageous: fifteen-year-old Harvey Cheyne, sick from a cigar and disoriented by the thick Grand Banks fog, falls from the deck of a transatlantic liner and is rescued by the Portuguese fisherman Manuel."

memes.label: Harvey Rescued
memes.description: The moment when Manuel, a Portuguese doryman from the schooner *We're Here*, pulls the half-drowned Harvey Cheyne from the North Atlantic after the boy is washed overboard from a transatlantic liner.

memes.label: Hatch
memes.description: The deck hatches of the We're Here — the main-hatch between the masts and the fore-hatch giving access to the hold, crew quarters, and the galley below.

memes.label: Hook
memes.description: The fish hook is the most fundamental tool of the cod fisherman in Captains Courageous. From the simple hand-line hook to the hundreds of hooks on a trawl line, the hook is central to every fishing operation on the Grand Banks.

memes.label: The Ireston Incident
aliases: the tarring and feathering of Ben Ireson

memes.label: Jacob Boiler
memes.description: Jacob Boiler, a Moravian preacher whose memory and influence surfaces among the fishermen aboard the We're Here in Captains Courageous, particularly through Penn's religious episodes.

memes.label: Jennie Cushman
memes.description: A Gloucester fishing schooner involved in an incident that became a standing joke among the Grand Banks fleet.

memes.label: Jib
memes.description: The jib is a triangular headsail set forward of the foremast on the fishing schooners in Captains Courageous. Together with the jib-boom that extends it, the jib is essential for maneuvering and speed on the Grand Banks.

memes.label: "Johnstown"
memes.description: "Johnstown, Pennsylvania — site of the 1889 flood disaster, referenced aboard the We're Here as Jacob Boiler's traumatic memory"

memes.label: "Kelleg"
memes.description: "A large stone used as a makeshift anchor, tied with rope instead of a proper iron anchor — a mark of poverty or incompetence on the Grand Banks, where every dory in the fleet can recognize a kelleg from far off and knows what it means."

memes.label: Le Have
memes.description: A port and fishing bank on the southern coast of Nova Scotia, part of the chain of Atlantic fishing grounds worked by the Gloucester fleet.

memes.label: "Le Have Bank"
memes.description: "A fishing bank off the coast of Nova Scotia, east of Halifax — another destination in the Gloucester fleet's seasonal rotation, known to Disko Troop and his contemporaries."

memes.label: Little Penn
memes.description: The nickname of Pennsylvania Pratt, a deaf crew member aboard the We're Here, known for his diminutive stature and hard work despite physical frailty.

memes.label: Liverpool
memes.description: A port town on the south shore of Nova Scotia, whose fishermen appear in the Gloucester memorial tablet as casualties of the North Atlantic fishery in *Captains Courageous*.

memes.label: "Long Jack"
memes.description: "A veteran fisherman aboard the We're Here — tall, experienced, and possessed of a melodious bellow that carries across the water — who watches Harvey Cheyne's progress with a critical but approving eye."

memes.label: Los Angeles
memes.description: A California city that serves as one of the key locations in the Cheyne family's western business empire, home to the yacht Constance and a base for the railroads that speed Harvey Cheyne's journey home.

memes.label: Madeira
memes.description: A Portuguese archipelago in the North Atlantic, home island of Manuel the Portuguese crewman aboard the *We're Here*, whose memories of Madeira provide some of the novel's most lyrical passages.

memes.label: Maine
memes.description: A New England state whose coastal communities and fishing culture form part of the broader maritime world of *Captains Courageous*, appearing in the narrative through rivalries with Massachusetts fishermen and references to its distinctive regional character.

memes.label: "Manuel"
memes.description: "A Portuguese fisherman aboard the We're Here who rescues Harvey Cheyne from the sea — a man of brilliant smiles, astonishing physical energy, and legendary skill with a splitting knife."

memes.label: Marblehead
memes.description: A historic Massachusetts seaport north of Boston, known in the novel for its distinctive fishing schooners and as the setting of the Ben Ireson tale — one of the oral stories told aboard the *We're Here*.

memes.label: Mariner
memes.description: A mariner is a seafarer, a person who works and lives at sea. In Captains Courageous, the term carries layers of meaning — from the ironic "ancient mariner" applied to young Harvey's pretensions, to the genuine respect accorded to experienced fishermen like Disko Troop.

memes.label: Maritime Folklore
memes.description: The body of beliefs, superstitions, and oral traditions shared among the fishermen of the Grand Banks fleet, reflecting centuries of seafaring culture.

memes.label: Mast
memes.description: The masts of the fishing schooner We're Here, a two-masted Gloucester vessel with a stump-foremast and mainmast, carrying the riding-sail and fly that guide her on the Grand Banks.

memes.label: "Memorial Day"
memes.description: "Memorial Day celebration in Gloucester, where Harvey Cheyne's family attends before departing"

memes.label: "Memorial Service for the Drowned"
memes.description: "A solemn ceremony held in Gloucester at the end of the fishing season to honor the fishermen lost at sea — a communal ritual that underscores the mortal danger of the Grand Banks fishery and the tight bonds of the fishing community."

memes.label: Memorial Tablet
memes.description: The Gloucester memorial tradition honoring lost fishermen, observed on Memorial Day with a collection for widows and orphans.

memes.label: Miquelon
memes.description: A French island territory south of Newfoundland, home to a fishing fleet whose vessels appear in the North Atlantic fishing grounds and interact with the *We're Here*'s crew.

memes.label: "Miss Kinzey"
memes.description: "Miss Kinzey is Harvey Cheyne Sr.'s stenographer, a capable and spirited young woman from Milwaukee who manages the telegraph correspondence during the frantic cross-country dash to rescue Harvey in Chapter 9 of Captains Courageous."

memes.label: Mr. Cheyne
memes.description: Harvey Cheyne Sr., millionaire railroad magnate and father of Harvey Cheyne, whose race across America to rescue his son drives the novel's final chapters.

memes.label: Navigation
memes.description: The art and skill of navigating a vessel at sea, practiced by the fishermen of the Grand Banks fleet using traditional knowledge rather than formal training.

memes.label: "New York"
memes.description: "The great city that Harvey Cheyne calls home before his transformation — a world of luxury hotels, private railcars, and millionaire society, sharply contrasted with the honest labor of the Grand Banks."

memes.label: Newfoundland
memes.description: A large Canadian island at the eastern edge of the Gulf of St. Lawrence, whose rugged coastline and fishing communities form the northern backdrop to the Grand Banks fishery in which the *We're Here* operates.

memes.label: "Nippers"
memes.description: "Woolen circlets worn around the palms to protect a fisherman's hands from the cutting bite of a cod-line when a heavy fish strikes and runs — essential personal gear for every dory fisherman on the Grand Banks."

memes.label: North Atlantic
memes.description: The ocean region where the story opens, with Harvey Cheyne aboard a transatlantic liner in thick fog.

memes.label: "Nova Scotia"
memes.description: "Nova Scotia, the Canadian Maritime province whose fishermen formed a significant portion of the Grand Banks fleet in Captains Courageous — a region of rocky coasts, fishing villages, and a proud seafaring culture distinct from the Gloucestermen who worked beside them."

memes.label: Ocean
memes.description: The Atlantic Ocean is the vast setting of Captains Courageous, where the Grand Banks fishing fleet operates and where Harvey Cheyne undergoes his transformation from spoiled rich boy to capable young man.

memes.label: Index of Organizations
memes.description: Index of all organizations in the Captains Courageous wiki — businesses, fleets, and societies that shape the novel's world.

memes.label: "Penn"
memes.description: "A small, scholarly fisherman aboard the We're Here known as 'Pennsylvania' — a gentle man of learning and hidden tragedy who reads Gaelic, knows the classics, and toils beyond his strength among the salt and cod, embodying Kipling's theme of loss and quiet dignity."

memes.label: Index of Persons
memes.description: Complete index of all characters in the Captains Courageous wiki, sorted alphabetically by name.

memes.label: Index of Places
memes.description: Complete geographic index of all locations in the Captains Courageous wiki — ports, fishing grounds, landmarks, cities, and other places.

memes.label: "Ports of the Gloucester Fleet"
memes.description: "A geographic survey of the ports, harbors, and coastal landmarks that defined the world of the Gloucester fishing fleet in Captains Courageous."

memes.label: Provincetown
memes.description: A fishing port at the tip of Cape Cod, Massachusetts, whose fishermen form part of the multi-origin crews that man the Grand Banks fleet in the novel.

memes.label: Railroad
memes.description: Railroads are the source of the Cheyne family fortune in Captains Courageous, representing the world of wealth, power, and industrial capitalism that Harvey Cheyne leaves behind when he falls into the sea and into the fishing life of the Grand Banks.

memes.label: Dead Reckoning
memes.description: The method of determining a vessel's position at sea by calculating course steered, speed, and time elapsed from the last known position — the primary navigation technique used by Disko Troop on the Grand Banks

memes.label: Rescue at Sea
memes.description: The act of saving Harvey Cheyne from drowning after he falls overboard from a transatlantic steamer, performed by the Portuguese fisherman Manuel in his dory, which sets the entire course of the novel

memes.label: "Riding Sail"
memes.description: "A small triangular sail set on the mainmast of a fishing schooner while at anchor, designed to keep the vessel's head to the wind and reduce rolling in the open sea."

memes.label: Rigging
memes.description: The system of ropes, cables, and chains that supports a schooner's masts and controls its sails, forming the web through which fishermen move as they work the vessel

memes.label: Sable Island
memes.description: A narrow, crescent-shaped island off the coast of Nova Scotia, notorious among North Atlantic fishermen as a navigational hazard and known historically as the "Graveyard of the Atlantic."

memes.label: Sail
memes.description: Sails are the primary means of propulsion for the fishing schooners and other vessels in Captains Courageous, capturing the wind to move ships across the Grand Banks.

memes.label: "Salt Cod"
memes.description: "Cod that has been split, salted, and packed in layers in a schooner's hold — the primary product of the Grand Banks fishery and a staple food traded across the Atlantic world in the late 19th century."

memes.label: San Diego
memes.description: The Cheyne family's primary residence on the West Coast, where Mr. Cheyne's business empire is headquartered.

memes.label: San Francisco
memes.description: The California port city from which Cheyne Sr.'s tea-clippers sail to Yokohama, and a node in the vast commercial network that underpins the Cheyne family fortune in *Captains Courageous*.

memes.label: Santa Fe Railroad
memes.description: The Atchison, Topeka, and Santa Fe Railroad, the western rail line that carries Harvey Cheyne Sr.'s private car across the continent in the climactic cross-country dash of Captains Courageous.

memes.label: "Schooner"
memes.description: "A fore-and-aft rigged sailing vessel with two or more masts, used as the mother ship for dory-based cod fishing on the Grand Banks in the late 19th century."

memes.label: Sea Shanties
memes.description: Traditional sailors' work songs sung aboard the fishing schooners of the Gloucester fleet, passed down through oral tradition among the fishermen of the Grand Banks.

memes.label: Sixteenth Street
memes.description: Sixteenth Street in Chicago, a critical railroad junction where the Santa Fe line transfers Harvey Cheyne Sr.'s private car to the New York Limited in the cross-country dash of Captains Courageous.

memes.label: Sounding
memes.description: The process of measuring water depth using a weighted line, an essential navigation and fishing technique on the Grand Banks where the seafloor depth determines the presence of cod.

memes.label: St. Malo
memes.description: A historic port city in Brittany, France, that served as the home port for many French fishing vessels working the Grand Banks, including the Miquelon boats encountered by the *We're Here* in *Captains Courageous*.

memes.label: Steamer
memes.description: Steam-powered ocean-going vessels that plied the North Atlantic in the late 19th century, including the passenger liner from which Harvey Cheyne falls overboard at the start of the novel

memes.label: The Cabin (We're Here)
memes.description: The main cabin of the fishing schooner We're Here, serving as the crew's living quarters, dining area, and social hub during fishing voyages.

memes.label: "The Caplin Run"
memes.description: "A spectacular nighttime event when a vast school of caplin — small silvery bait fish — surfaces near the We're Here, triggering a frantic, exhilarating scramble as every dory in the fleet races to fill barrels with the precious bait before the school vanishes."

memes.label: "The Gale at Sea"
memes.description: "A violent storm at sea that tests the seamanship of the We're Here's crew and furthers Harvey Cheyne's education in the realities of life on the Grand Banks."

memes.label: "The Race Back to Gloucester"
memes.description: "The We're Here's final voyage from the Grand Banks back to Gloucester — an informal race among the fishing fleet that tests seamanship, speed, and nerve, and brings Harvey Cheyne's months at sea to a triumphant conclusion."

memes.label: "Tom Platt"
memes.description: "A scarred former man-o'-war's man aboard the We're Here, veteran of the old Navy who served on the Ohio — a by-the-book sailor whose experience and salty wisdom provide Harvey Cheyne with a window into a vanishing maritime world."

memes.label: Topsail
memes.description: The topsail of the We're Here — a sail set above the main course, used in lighter winds, requiring heavy crew work, and visible for miles as a signal on the open Banks.

memes.label: "Trawl Line"
memes.description: "A long ground-line carrying hundreds of baited hooks, coiled in tubs and set from dories on the Grand Banks as an alternative to hand-lining — harder on the back but capable of catching more fish in a single set, and a window into the tension between efficiency and tradition on the Banks."

memes.label: "Uncle Salters"
memes.description: "A farmer-turned-fisherman aboard the We're Here — Disko Troop's brother — whose clumsy oarsmanship and painstakingly slow fish-counting make him the frequent target of Long Jack's exasperated humor."

memes.label: "Virgin Rock"
memes.description: "A prominent underwater rock formation on the Grand Banks, rising to within twenty feet of the surface — a crucial navigational landmark and turning-point of the fishing cruise, known to every Gloucester fisherman as a gathering place for the fleet and a dangerous breaker when the swell runs."

memes.label: Voyage
memes.description: A voyage is a long journey by sea, and in Captains Courageous the fishing voyage is the central framework of the narrative — the seasonal trip to the Grand Banks that transforms Harvey Cheyne from a spoiled boy into a capable seaman.

memes.label: Weather
memes.description: Weather conditions on the Grand Banks — fog, gales, and calm — that shape every aspect of life aboard a fishing schooner and the fates of those who work her.

memes.label: We're Here
memes.description: The We're Here, a Gloucester fishing schooner captained by Disko Troop, serves as the primary setting for Chapters 2 through 8 of Captains Courageous and the vessel that rescues Harvey Cheyne from the Grand Banks.

memes.label: Ship's Wheel
memes.description: The ship's wheel of the We're Here, from which Captain Disko Troop steers the schooner through fog, gales, and the crowded waters of the Grand Banks fishing fleet.

memes.label: "Wind"
memes.description: "The driving force of all sailing on the Grand Banks — winds governed the movement and fortunes of the fishing fleet, from the light airs that barely filled a riding-sail to the gales that sent schooners running for shelter."

memes.label: Windlass
memes.description: A mechanical device used aboard fishing schooners for raising and lowering the anchor, consisting of a horizontal barrel rotated by handspikes or brakes — the engine of the ship's morning ritual and a symbol of the coordinated labor that defines life on the Banks.
